import Mathlib
open Classical

/-! The main lemma for interpolation through local tableaux
This is a generalization of Lemma 7.21 in the book, and it's used to prove the
interpolation property for the left side of the main disjunction. -/
lemma localInterpolantStep {L R nlf} (lt : LocalTableau (L, R, some (Sum.inl nlf)))
    (endθs : ∀ Y ∈ endNodesOf lt, PartInterpolant Y)
    : PartInterpolant (L, R, some (Sum.inl nlf)) := by
  cases lt
  case byLocalRule lra nexts X_def =>
    subst X_def
    apply localInterpolantStep lra
    intro Y Y_in
    have IH := LocalTableau.interpolant (nexts Y Y_in)
    exact IH (fun Z Z_in_end => endθs _ (endNodeOfChild_to_endNode lra nexts rfl Y_in Z_in_end))
  case sim Xbas =>
    apply endθs X
    simp [endNodesOf]

/-! The main lemma for interpolation through Pdl rules applied to free nodes

  cases r
  case loadL in_L notBox Y_def =>
    use θ
    subst Y_def
    rcases θ_ip_Y with ⟨hYvoc, hYL, hYR⟩
    refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
    · intro x x_in
      specialize hYvoc x_in
      simp only [jvoc, List.fvoc, Vocab.fromList, Sequent.left_eq, Olf.L_inl, unload_boxes,
        LoadFormula.unload, List.map_append, List.map_cons, Formula.voc, List.map_nil,
        List.toFinset_append, List.toFinset_cons, List.toFinset_nil, insert_empty_eq,
        Finset.union_singleton, Finset.sup_insert, id_eq, Finset.sup_eq_union', Sequent.right_eq,
        Olf.R_inl, List.append_nil, Finset.mem_inter, Finset.mem_union, Finset.mem_sup,

/-! ## Interpolants for Local Tableau -/

def LocalTableau.interpolant (ltX : LocalTableau X)
    (endθs : ∀ Y ∈ endNodesOf ltX, PartInterpolant Y)
    : PartInterpolant X := by
  cases ltX
  case byLocalRule lra nexts X_def =>
    subst X_def
    apply localInterpolantStep lra
    intro Y Y_in
    have IH := LocalTableau.interpolant (nexts Y Y_in)
    exact IH (fun Z Z_in_end => endθs _ (endNodeOfChild_to_endNode lra nexts rfl Y_in Z_in_end))
  case sim Xbas =>
    apply endθs X
    simp [endNodesOf]

  unfold exitsOf at *
  rcases lr_nlf with (nlf|nlf)
  · cases tab
    case loc nbas lt nrep next =>

        Sequent.isLoaded, decide_true, ↓reduceIte, List.mem_flatMap, List.mem_attach, List.mem_map,
        true_and, Subtype.exists, Sum.swap_inr] at *
      rcases s_in with ⟨Yf, Yf_in, e, e_in, def_s⟩
      rcases exists_flip_of_endNodesOf Yf_in with ⟨Y, def_Yf, Y_in⟩
      subst def_Yf def_s
      rcases Y with ⟨LY, RY, (_|lr_nlf_Y)⟩
      · simp [Sequent.flip] at e
        simp_all [Sequent.flip]

import Mathlib


open Finset


/-! ## Basic definitions and lemmas -/

noncomputable def endNodesOf {X} {t : Tableau X} : List (Sequent X) :=
  match t with
  | .lrep _ => [] -- Repeats have no end nodes
  | .loc _ _ lt next => (endNodesOf lt).attach.flatMap (fun ⟨Y,Y_in⟩ =>
                                  (endNodesOf (next Y Y_in)).map (fun n => ⟨Y, Y_in, n⟩))
  | .pdl _ _ _ next => (endNodesOf next).map (fun n => ⟨_, _, n⟩) -- PDL rules have no end nodes

lemma endNodesOf_spec {X} {t : Tableau X} {Y : Sequent X} : Y ∈ endNodesOf t ↔ ∃ n, t = n ∧ Y = n.1 := by
  simp only [endNodesOf, List.mem_append, List.mem_cons, List.mem_nil, exists_eq_right_right, true_and, exists_eq_right]
  <;> aesop

noncomputable def exitsOf {X} {t : Tableau X} : List (Sequent X) :=
  match t with
  | .lrep _ => [] -- Repeats have no exits
  | .loc _ _ lt next => (endNodesOf lt).attach.flatMap (fun ⟨Y,Y_in⟩ =>
                                  (exitsOf (next Y Y_in)).map (fun e => ⟨Y, Y_in, e⟩))
  | .pdl _ _ _ next => (exitsOf next).map (fun e => ⟨_, _, e⟩) -- PDL rules have no exits

lemma exitsOf_spec {X} {t : Tableau X} {Y : Sequent X} : Y ∈ exitsOf t ↔ ∃ n, t = n ∧ Y = n.1 ∧ n.2 := by
  simp only [exitsOf, List.mem_append, List.mem_cons, List.mem_nil, exists_eq_right_right, true_and, exists_eq_right]
  <;> aesop

noncomputable def isExitIn {X} {t : Tableau X} {Y : Sequent X} : Prop := Y ∈ exitsOf t

lemma isExitIn_spec {X} {t : Tableau X} {Y : Sequent X} : isExitIn t Y ↔ Y ∈ exitsOf t := by
  simp only [isExitIn, exitsOf]

/-- A local rule application, where we have a local tableaux `lt` applied to a set of hypotheses `H` to produce a new state, with the right side of the new state being `Y`.
   This is used in the main theorem to construct a sequence of states.
   The state is represented as `⟨L, R, O, H⟩` where `L, R` are lists of formulas, `O` is an option of a sum of `None` or a `NegLoadFormula`, and `H` is a list of `Sequent`s (the hypotheses).
   The local rule is applied to the right side of the current state's right side, which is `Y`.
   The new state is `⟨L', R', O', H'⟩` where `L', R', O'` are computed by the local rule, and `H'` is the original `H` with `Y` appended.
   The local rule is specified by `⟨Y, lrule⟩` where `lrule` is the rule itself.
   The resulting state is a local tableaux followed by the `next` sequence. -/
def localRuleStep (H : List (Sequent X)) (Y : Sequent X) (lrule : LocalRule X Y)
    : Tableau ((lrule.1 :: H)) X :=
  let ⟨L, R, O, H⟩ := lrule.2
  match lrule.1 with
  | .left => .loc (lrule.2.1.1) (lrule.2.1.2) (L, R, O) (fun Y_in => .loc (lrule.2.2.1 Y_in) H Y_in) -- Left rule
  | .right => .loc (lrule.2.1.1) (lrule.2.1.2) (L, R, O) (fun Y_in => .loc (lrule.2.2.2 Y_in) H Y_in) -- Right rule

  cases r

    rcases θ_ip_Y with ⟨hYvoc, hYL, hYR⟩
    refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
    · intro x x_in

      simp only [jvoc, List.fvoc, Vocab.fromList, Sequent.left_eq, Olf.L_inl, unload_boxes,
        LoadFormula.unload, List.map_append, List.map_cons, Formula.voc, List.map_nil,
        List.toFinset_append, List.toFinset_cons, List.toFinset_nil, insert_empty_eq,

        Olf.R_inl, List.append_nil, Finset.mem_inter, Finset.mem_union, Finset.mem_sup,
        List.mem_toFinset, List.mem_map, exists_exists_and_eq_and] at hYvoc
      simp only [jvoc, List.fvoc, Vocab.fromList, Sequent.left_eq, Olf.L_none, List.append_nil,
        Sequent.right_eq, Olf.R_none, Finset.mem_inter, Finset.mem_sup, List.mem_toFinset,
        List.mem_map, id_eq, exists_exists_and_eq_and]
      rcases hYvoc with ⟨x_from, ⟨φ, φ_inR, x_from_φ⟩⟩
      constructor
      · rcases x_from with (hx|hx)
        · exact ⟨_, in_L, hx⟩
        · grind
      · use φ
    all_goals
      clear notBox Xfree
      simp at *
      grind
  case loadR in_R notBox Y_def=>
    use θ
    subst Y_def
    rcases θ_ip_Y with ⟨hYvoc, hYL, hYR⟩
    refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
    · intro x x_in
      specialize hYvoc x_in
      simp only [jvoc, List.fvoc, Vocab.fromList, Sequent.left_eq, Olf.L_inr, List.append_nil,
        Sequent.right_eq, Olf.R_inr, unload_boxes, LoadFormula.unload, List.map_append,
        List.map_cons, Formula.voc, List.map_nil, List.toFinset_append, List.toFinset_cons,
        List.toFinset_nil, insert_empty_eq, Finset.union_singleton, Finset.sup_insert, id_eq,
        Finset.sup_eq_union', Finset.mem_inter, Finset.mem_sup, List.mem_toFinset, List.mem_map,
        exists_exists_and_eq_and, Finset.mem_union] at hYvoc
      simp only [jvoc, List.fvoc, Vocab.fromList, Sequent.left_eq, Olf.L_none, List.append_nil,
        Sequent.right_eq, Olf.R_none, Finset.mem_inter, Finset.mem_sup, List.mem_toFinset,

      rcases hYvoc with ⟨⟨φ, φ_inR, x_from_φ⟩, x_from⟩
      constructor
      · use φ
      · rcases x_from with (hx|hx)
        · exact ⟨_, in_R, hx⟩
        · grind
    all_goals
      clear notBox Xfree
      simp at *
      grind
  all_goals
    exfalso
    subst_eqs
